question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests to GET /api/v1/contracts/results/{transactionIdOrHash}/opcodes with stack=true&memory=true&storage=true all enabled simultaneously, causing the EVM replay in opcodeService.processOpcodeCall() to consume unbounded CPU and heap memory across multiple threads, degrading or crashing 30%+ of mirror node instances?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: throttleManager.throttleOpcodeRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that throttleOpcodeRequest() uses a single global opcodeRateLimitBucket shared across all clients (default 1 RPS) by distributing requests across multiple source IPs or connections, bypassing the per-instance rate limit and sustaining enough EVM replay load to degrade 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with stack=true, memory=true, and storage=true simultaneously for a historically large contract transaction, causing opcodeService.processOpcodeCall() to allocate a response payload so large it exhausts JVM heap memory on the mirror node, causing an OutOfMemoryError that crashes the process?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: validateAcceptEncodingHeader()] Can an unprivileged external user bypass the gzip compression requirement in validateAcceptEncodingHeader() by sending Accept-Encoding: gzip,deflate or Accept-Encoding: GZIP (mixed case), causing the server to stream an uncompressed multi-megabyte opcode trace response that saturates network bandwidth and memory buffers on the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a valid request referencing a transaction that triggered a deeply recursive EVM contract call, causing the EVM replay inside processOpcodeCall() to consume unbounded stack depth and CPU time, leading to a StackOverflowError or sustained CPU saturation that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user identify a historically gas-heavy transaction (up to maxGasLimit=15000000) and repeatedly replay it via the opcodes endpoint, where each replay triggers a full EVM re-execution consuming proportional CPU, causing sustained CPU exhaustion across mirror node instances handling 30%+ of network traffic?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: throttleManager.throttleOpcodeRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that throttleOpcodeRequest() is called after validateAcceptEncodingHeader() but before processOpcodeCall(), meaning a request that passes header validation but is throttled still consumes a token from the opcodeRateLimitBucket, allowing an attacker to drain the bucket with cheap invalid requests and then submit a single expensive replay when the bucket refills?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with an extremely long transactionIdOrHash path variable (e.g., thousands of characters) that passes initial Spring path binding but causes excessive processing in TransactionIdOrHashParameter.valueOf(), leading to CPU or memory exhaustion in the parsing layer across many concurrent requests?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-IP or per-client rate limiting in throttleOpcodeRequest() — which only enforces a global server-side bucket — by opening many parallel HTTP/1.1 keep-alive connections from different IPs, each consuming one token per second, to sustain a continuous stream of expensive EVM replays that degrades node processing capacity by 30%+?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: opcodeService.processOpcodeCall()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a request for a transaction whose EVM replay produces an extremely large OpcodeContext.opcodes list (e.g., millions of opcodes from a loop-heavy contract), causing the ArrayList in OpcodeContext.addOpcodes() to grow unboundedly and exhaust JVM heap memory, crashing the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where the transactionIdOrHash path variable is a valid 32-byte hex hash that does not exist in the database, causing the service to perform a full database lookup on every request, and by sending many such requests per second (within the throttle window), exhaust database connection pool resources and degrade node availability?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: validateAcceptEncodingHeader()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with Accept-Encoding: gzip;q=0 (gzip with quality factor zero, meaning gzip is explicitly rejected) that still passes the validateAcceptEncodingHeader() check because it contains the substring 'gzip', causing the server to attempt gzip compression on a response the client refuses, leading to connection errors or resource waste?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the opcodes endpoint performs a full EVM re-execution (noted in the Javadoc as potentially taking 'significant amount of time') by sending requests for the most computationally expensive historical transactions, holding server threads in a blocked state long enough to exhaust the Spring MVC thread pool and deny service to 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with stack=true and memory=true for a transaction that executed a contract with large memory allocation opcodes (MSTORE/MLOAD in a loop), causing the EVM replay to allocate gigabytes of memory in the OpcodeContext, exhausting JVM heap and crashing the mirror node process?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: throttleManager.throttleOpcodeRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition in the bucket4j opcodeRateLimitBucket.tryConsume(1) check — where two concurrent requests both read the bucket as non-empty before either decrements it — to bypass the 1 RPS throttle and submit two simultaneous expensive EVM replays, doubling the resource consumption per second?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with storage=true for a transaction that interacted with a contract having a very large number of storage slots, causing the EVM replay to load and serialize all storage slot data into the OpcodeContext, producing a response that exhausts both memory and network bandwidth on the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the requestTimeout is 10000ms (10 seconds) and the EVM replay can take 'significant time', by sending requests that each hold a server thread for the full 10-second timeout, exhausting the Tomcat/Netty thread pool with only 30 concurrent connections and denying service to other requests?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where the transactionIdOrHash is a valid transaction ID (shard.realm.num-seconds-nanos format) pointing to a transaction with maximum valid start timestamp values (Long.MAX_VALUE seconds), causing integer overflow or unexpected behavior in convertToNanosMax() inside OpcodeServiceImpl, leading to incorrect database queries or exceptions that crash the handler?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of authentication on the /api/v1/contracts/results/{transactionIdOrHash}/opcodes endpoint to send requests from multiple geographic regions simultaneously, each region consuming the global opcodeRateLimitBucket token, sustaining a continuous load of expensive EVM replays that degrades 30%+ of mirror node processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with memory=true for a transaction that executed MSTORE operations filling the maximum EVM memory (limited only by gas), causing the memory trace in OpcodeContext to serialize all memory snapshots at every opcode step, producing a response payload of hundreds of megabytes that exhausts the node's available memory?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: validateAcceptEncodingHeader()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a crafted Accept-Encoding header value of extreme length (e.g., 1MB of padding followed by 'gzip'), which passes the validateAcceptEncodingHeader() substring check but causes excessive string processing via acceptEncodingHeader.toLowerCase().contains('gzip'), consuming CPU proportional to header length across many concurrent requests?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the OpcodeContext is initialized with capacity (int) params.getGas() / 3 in OpcodeServiceImpl, and by targeting a transaction with gasLimit=15000000 (max), cause an ArrayList pre-allocation of 5,000,000 entries that immediately consumes significant heap memory per request, enabling heap exhaustion with only a few concurrent requests?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests for transactions that involve deeply nested contract calls (CREATE2 chains), causing the EVM replay to recursively instantiate contract execution contexts, consuming thread stack space and heap proportional to call depth, leading to StackOverflowError or OOM that crashes the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the opcodes endpoint has no response size limit, by targeting a transaction that produces millions of opcodes, causing the JSON serialization of OpcodesResponse to consume unbounded CPU and memory during serialization, blocking the response thread and degrading node throughput?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a transactionIdOrHash that is a valid hex hash matching multiple contract transaction hash entries in the database, causing the service to load and process multiple transaction records, multiplying the database query load and EVM replay cost per single HTTP request?
